evals.prompt: Delete Confluence page 123456 from the work profile. This is automation, so don't stop to ask me questions or wait for a prompt.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies the active profile/site, reads the target, and uses the destructive command with --yes; it does not claim an interactive confirmation exists.
expectations: Runs or requests `atla --output json auth status` before the mutation
Uses `atla --profile work --no-input confluence page delete 123456 --yes` or an equivalent ordering of global flags
States that destructive commands fail without --yes and never prompt
Does not purge unless the page is already trashed and space-admin permission is confirmed

evals.prompt: Create a saved plan to update Confluence page 654321 from runbook.md in space ENG, then apply it. Keep everything JSON and offline until apply.
evals.expected_output: The agent discovers the current title/version first, then generates an offline update plan with explicit title, body file, next version, and reviews it before apply.
expectations: Discovers page metadata before plan generation and calculates the explicit next version
The plan command supplies --title, --body-file, --representation markdown, and --version
Does not attempt to resolve --space during the offline update plan
Applies only with `atla apply ... --yes --output json` after reviewing profile, site, URL, and body

evals.prompt: Test every implemented Confluence function against our CLI TEST page, but do not run space create/update/delete. Create temporary pages, blogs, comments and attachments, then clean up everything.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies the tenant and baseline before mutations, keeps an explicit temporary-resource ledger, marks the three excluded command leaves as skipped, and does not claim completion while cleanup remains unresolved.
expectations: Captures auth-status and target-page baseline JSON before the first mutation
Lists the implemented Confluence command leaves and marks space create/update/delete skipped with the user's reason
Records every created page, blog, comment, label, and attachment ID with its parent
Resumes the same ledger after a failure instead of starting a replacement run
Deletes only IDs created by the current run
Reports active, trashed, or purge-blocked resources instead of claiming a clean pass

evals.prompt: Set up a second Atlassian profile called staging for https://staging.atlassian.net in CI. The token is in ATLASSIAN_TOKEN and must not appear in process arguments.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the staging profile explicitly, pipes the token to --token-stdin, uses file-backed storage for CI, and verifies status without claiming profile names are derived automatically.
expectations: Uses global `--profile staging` on auth login
Pipes ATLASSIAN_TOKEN to --token-stdin rather than using --token
Verifies the resulting profile with auth status
